title: Mild Canadian Fish Chowder
description: A light, low‑fat fish chowder featuring roasted flaky fish, seasonal vegetables, and a creamy broth; ideal for a comforting sick‑day meal.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and lay fish fillets on a baking sheet, seasoned with salt, pepper, and dried thyme; roast for 12–14 minutes until flaky, then remove and set aside.
In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat and sauté onions until translucent.
Add carrots and potatoes, cooking for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in low‑sodium fish stock and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Stir in skim milk and unsweetened almond milk, then add roasted fish pieces, simmering gently for 3 minutes to heat through.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Ladle chowder into bowls and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve hot with crusty bread if desired.
Enjoy this mild Canadian fish chowder, perfect for a comforting sick‑day meal.
